parentAction.completionRule: A bounded enumerator reproduces all six restored menus exactly with no omitted smaller representatives, the original generator is restored, or a precise blocker names the missing ordering proof.

boundedEnumerator.scope: Restored finite SIX_PREFIX_NINETEEN through SIX_PREFIX_TWENTY_FOUR parameter surfaces only.

algorithm.description: Enumerate distinct right-half CRT progressions for anchors 82,132,182; enumerate n <= the restored menu representative bound for each progression; directly test anchors 7,32,57 for unused square witnesses; keep only distinct six-square tuple rows.
algorithm.tieBreakPolicy: Sort by representative ascending, then by the stored tupleKey display string ascending, then take the first menu limit rows.
algorithm.whyBounded: The representative bound is the maximum representative in each restored menu. This proves containment/no-smaller-omission for the finite restored surfaces without using a naive 23^6 Cartesian CRT search.

provedForRestoredFiniteSurface: Every restored menu row in SIX_PREFIX_NINETEEN through SIX_PREFIX_TWENTY_FOUR is produced by the bounded CRT enumerator.
No enumerated row with representative smaller than the restored menu boundary is omitted from any restored menu.
The duplicate/equal-representative tie policy is recovered: representative ascending, tupleKey display string ascending, then first limit rows.
The bounded enumerator exactly reproduces all six restored finite menus under that policy.
The naive all-tuple Cartesian CRT probe is replaced by a bounded right-half CRT plus direct left-half witness test.
remainingOpen: SIX_PREFIX_TWENTY_FOUR has one additional enumerated tuple at the same boundary representative 1837022639, and the recovered tie policy excludes it by the finite limit.
The original family-menu generator/source is still not restored.
No symbolic all-future relevant-pair recurrence or finite Q partition is proved.

deterministicBoundary.blockedInput: A symbolic all-future relevant-pair recurrence, finite Q partition, original generator source, or theorem showing the bounded restored finite enumerator suffices for the universal mod-50 square-witness domain cover.
acceptedSuccessors: Promote the bounded CRT enumerator as an exact finite restored-menu replay theorem with all-future recurrence explicitly open.
Restore the original family-menu generator and compare its ordering/source rule with the bounded enumerator.
Prove a symbolic relevant-pair recurrence or finite Q partition independent of these finite menu snapshots.
deterministicBoundary.forbiddenShortcut: Do not treat exact restored finite-menu reproduction as original generator restoration, universal relevant-pair enumeration, finite Q partition, or all-N proof.

proofBoundary: The bounded CRT enumerator exactly reproduces the restored finite menus, including duplicate/equal-representative ordering, under the recovered tupleKey-string tie policy. It does not restore the original generator, prove a symbolic recurrence, close contrast-only recombination, or prove Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Promote the exact bounded CRT menu enumerator as a finite restored-menu replay theorem, then either derive a symbolic all-future relevant-pair recurrence/finite Q partition or emit the next blocker at that universal boundary.
